title: Discovery UI: Chain Dashboard + Audit Viewer (events, indexer lag, incidents, audit log export)
description: Implement the Chain Dashboard and Audit Viewer pages. The Chain Dashboard shows blockchain connectivity status, contract addresses, last processed block, indexer lag, and recent on-chain events. Alert flags highlight critical incidents (issuer disabled, emergency revoke). The Audit Viewer provides a searchable, filterable view of all Discovery Service audit events with paging, JSONL export, and tamper-detection indicator.
objectives: Chain Dashboard: network/chainId, contract addresses, RPC status, last_block, indexer_lag, last_event_ts
Events view: table with event_type, tx_hash (truncated+link), block, actor_hash, payload summary
Alert flags: issuer_disabled_recently, status_stale_detected, emergency_revoke, indexer_lag_high
Audit Viewer: table with filters (actor, action, target, time range), pagination, streaming cursor
JSONL export: download audit events as JSONL file for offline analysis
Tamper detection: show indicator if audit hash chain verification fails

steps.title: Chain Dashboard status cards
steps.detail: File `src/pages/ChainPage.tsx`. API call: `GET /api/v1/chain/status` returns `{network_name, chain_id, rpc_endpoint_status: ok|unreachable|degraded, last_block: int, indexer_lag_blocks: int, last_event_ts: str, contract_addresses: {issuer_registry, trust_policy_registry, status_registry, service_registry}}`. Render as a 2-column grid of MUI `Card` components: (1) Network Info card: network_name, chain_id, RPC status chip. (2) Sync Status card: last_block, indexer_lag_blocks (color: green < 3, yellow 3-10, red > 10), last_event_ts. (3-6) Contract Cards: one per contract with address (truncated + copy button + Etherscan link if testnet). Auto-refresh every 30s using `setInterval` (clear on unmount).

steps.title: Alert flags panel
steps.detail: API call: `GET /api/v1/chain/alerts` returns `{alerts: [{type, severity, message, triggered_at, resolved: bool}]}`. Render as MUI `Alert` banners at top of Chain page. Alert types and styling: `issuer_disabled` â†’ severity=error, message includes issuer DID hash; `status_stale` â†’ severity=warning; `emergency_revoke` â†’ severity=error with `ErrorOutline` icon; `indexer_lag_high` â†’ severity=warning. Include 'Acknowledge' button on each alert (calls `PATCH /api/v1/chain/alerts/{id}/acknowledge`). Acknowledged alerts collapse but remain visible in 'Recent alerts' collapsible section for audit trail. Critical alerts (error severity) also shown as a notification badge on the 'Chain' nav item in sidebar.

steps.title: Chain events table
steps.detail: Component `ChainEventsTable` in `src/components/chain/`. API call: `GET /api/v1/chain/events?event_type=&page=&page_size=20`. Columns: block (right-aligned), event_type chip, tx_hash (first 10 chars + external link to block explorer `https://sepolia.etherscan.io/tx/{tx_hash}` for testnet), actor_hash (8 chars + tooltip), payload_summary (JSON key preview, truncated to 100 chars). Event type chips: `IssuerRegistered` (blue), `IssuerRevoked` (red), `PolicyUpdated` (orange), `StatusAnchored` (green), `ServiceRegistered` (teal). Filter by event_type via checkbox group. Click row: expand to show full payload JSON (prettified, keys highlighted).

steps.title: Audit Viewer page
steps.detail: File `src/pages/AuditPage.tsx`. API call: `GET /api/v1/audit/events?actor_did=&action=&target_id=&from_ts=&to_ts=&page=&page_size=50&format=json`. Filter controls row: actor_did text input, action multi-select (use AuditAction enum values from backend), target_id text, date range picker (MUI DateTimePicker). Results table: ts (absolute), action (chip colored by category: credential actions=blue, chain actions=orange, auth actions=red, config actions=grey), actor_did_hash (8 chars), target_id, env. Pagination with 'Load more' button (cursor-based: pass `cursor=last_event_hash` to next page). Total count shown in header: 'Showing 1-50 of 12,340 events'.

steps.title: JSONL export
steps.detail: Button 'Export JSONL' in Audit Viewer header. On click: calls `GET /api/v1/audit/events?{current_filters}&format=jsonl&stream=true` which returns NDJSON stream (per TASK-034). Use Fetch API with streaming body: `const reader = response.body.getReader()`, read chunks, concatenate into Blob, trigger download via `URL.createObjectURL`. Show progress indicator 'Downloading... N events'. IMPORTANT: limit export to 100,000 events per request (API enforces this too). If user's filters would return > 100,000: show warning 'Narrow your time range. Export is limited to 100,000 events.' before initiating download.

steps.title: Tamper detection indicator
steps.detail: Component `TamperDetectionStatus` in `src/components/audit/`. API call: `GET /api/v1/audit/tamper-check` returns `{status: ok|warning|error, last_verified_at, events_checked, broken_chain_at: str | null}`. Render in Audit page header as: (1) green shield icon if `status=ok`; (2) yellow warning icon if `status=warning` (unverified since > 1h) with message 'Hash chain not recently verified'; (3) red error icon if `status=error` with message 'Hash chain broken at event {broken_chain_at}. Contact security team immediately.' Refresh tamper check status every 5 minutes.

steps.title: Tests
steps.detail: Test files: `src/pages/__tests__/ChainPage.test.tsx` and `AuditPage.test.tsx`. Cases: (1) Chain status cards render with correct color for indexer lag; (2) Alert banner shows for issuer_disabled alert; (3) Acknowledge button calls correct API; (4) Chain events table renders with external Etherscan link; (5) Audit filter form submits search params to API; (6) JSONL export triggers file download; (7) Tamper check status renders correct icon for each status; (8) Pagination 'Load more' calls API with cursor parameter.

files.discovery-ui/src/pages/ChainPage.tsx: Chain dashboard with status cards and events
files.discovery-ui/src/api/audit.ts: Audit API client functions with JSONL export

security_constraints: Etherscan external links use `rel='noopener noreferrer'` to prevent tab hijacking
JSONL export is size-limited to prevent browser memory exhaustion from large downloads
Tamper detection error must not auto-dismiss â€” requires explicit admin acknowledgement
Audit filter inputs sanitized (no XSS via actor_did field) â€” API already escapes, UI uses textContent not innerHTML
Alert acknowledgement is logged as an audit event server-side â€” UI action is traceable
acceptance_criteria: Chain status cards show correct color-coded indexer lag indicator
Alert banners shown for active alerts, collapsible after acknowledgement
JSONL export successfully downloads and contains valid NDJSON
Tamper detection shows red error state when chain broken
Audit filter correctly queries API with all filter parameters
